- asserts a conclusion logically incompatible with the other's conclusion — the one claim can be true only if the other is false (rebuttal), OR
- undermines a premise the other relies on (undermining), OR
- shows the other's conclusion does not follow even if its premise is true (undercutting)

Pair pro29:pro7
  A=[pro29] (pro) David Frum , Daily Beast and CNN contributor , stated , " American children under age 15 were nine times more likely to die of a gun accident than children in other advanced wealthy countries … About 200 Americans go to emergency rooms every day with gunshot wounds . "
  B=[pro7] (pro) It is a terrible irony that the very same gun enthusiasts — many of whom see gun registration to be in infringement on personal liberty and fear an oppressive federal government — wish to impose what they fear onto others .

  A=[pro29] (pro) David Frum , Daily Beast and CNN contributor , stated , " American children under age 15 were nine times more likely to die of a gun accident than children in other advanced wealthy countries … About 200 Americans go to emergency rooms every day with gunshot wounds . "
  B=[pro71] (pro) These gun owners and sellers have hopelessly perverted the original intent of the Second Amendment.

Pair pro29:pro71b
  A=[pro29] (pro) David Frum , Daily Beast and CNN contributor , stated , " American children under age 15 were nine times more likely to die of a gun accident than children in other advanced wealthy countries … About 200 Americans go to emergency rooms every day with gunshot wounds . "
  B=[pro71b] (pro) These gun owners and sellers have expanded the guarantee of the right to keep and bear arms far beyond its original bounds.

  A=[pro29] (pro) David Frum , Daily Beast and CNN contributor , stated , " American children under age 15 were nine times more likely to die of a gun accident than children in other advanced wealthy countries … About 200 Americans go to emergency rooms every day with gunshot wounds . "

Pair pro29:pro58
  A=[pro29] (pro) David Frum , Daily Beast and CNN contributor , stated , " American children under age 15 were nine times more likely to die of a gun accident than children in other advanced wealthy countries … About 200 Americans go to emergency rooms every day with gunshot wounds . "
  B=[pro58] (pro) In 2007 the United States had 12,632 gun homicides (4.19 deaths per 100,000 people).

Pair pro29:pro58b
  A=[pro29] (pro) David Frum , Daily Beast and CNN contributor , stated , " American children under age 15 were nine times more likely to die of a gun accident than children in other advanced wealthy countries … About 200 Americans go to emergency rooms every day with gunshot wounds . "
  B=[pro58b] (pro) In 2007 the United States had 17,352 gun suicides (5.76 deaths per 100,000 people).

Pair pro29:pro36
  A=[pro29] (pro) David Frum , Daily Beast and CNN contributor , stated , " American children under age 15 were nine times more likely to die of a gun accident than children in other advanced wealthy countries … About 200 Americans go to emergency rooms every day with gunshot wounds . "

Pair pro29:pro36b
  A=[pro29] (pro) David Frum , Daily Beast and CNN contributor , stated , " American children under age 15 were nine times more likely to die of a gun accident than children in other advanced wealthy countries … About 200 Americans go to emergency rooms every day with gunshot wounds . "

Pair pro29:pro27
  A=[pro29] (pro) David Frum , Daily Beast and CNN contributor , stated , " American children under age 15 were nine times more likely to die of a gun accident than children in other advanced wealthy countries … About 200 Americans go to emergency rooms every day with gunshot wounds . "
  B=[pro27] (pro) Armed civilians are unlikely to stop crimes.

Pair pro29:pro27b
  A=[pro29] (pro) David Frum , Daily Beast and CNN contributor , stated , " American children under age 15 were nine times more likely to die of a gun accident than children in other advanced wealthy countries … About 200 Americans go to emergency rooms every day with gunshot wounds . "

direction    = exactly one of: A_ATTACKS_B / B_ATTACKS_A / NONE
attack_type  = one of: rebuttal / undercutting / undermining / NONE
  rebuttal:     the two conclusions are logically incompatible — they negate the SAME
                proposition, so one claim is true only if the other is false. Merely
                taking the opposing stance, addressing a different sub-topic, or
                "arguing for the other side" is NOT a rebuttal; there must be a direct

  undermining:  attacker directly attacks a premise the target relies on

confidence   = integer 0-100: probability that ANY attack exists between the two arguments
  (0 = definitely no attack, 100 = definitely attack)
  If direction=NONE, confidence MUST be 0.
